reviews.author: Michal Hocko
reviews.summary: Reviewer Michal Hocko did not address the technical content of the patch, instead mentioning that they got sidetracked and will respond later.
reviews.raw_body:
Sorry, I got side tracked and didn't get to your follow up yet. I will
try sometimes next week. Please poke if I do not respond.

-- 
Michal Hocko
SUSE Labs

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory accounting system to provide per-memcg-lruvec and per-node statistics for percpu allocations. It converts MEMCG_PERCPU_B from a memcg_stat_item to a node_stat_item, allowing for separate tracking of per-node and per-memcg-lruvec statistics. The patch also exposes mod_memcg_lruvec_state to enable external access to these statistics. Additionally, it simplifies the accounting of object pointers by not charging them against any specific lruvec, instead treating them as part of the pcpu objects' size.
